title: Cherub Cherub everywhere
wikitext:
[]: Cherub... Cherub everywhere             Quote Post by Gtamor » Wed Sep 12, 2012 6:05 pm (not to be confused with putti ) The very first thing we see in NGE (right at the beginning of the intro) is an image of a cherub : which seems to be designed very similar to this (especially with the eyes, will get back to that): examples of other images of cherub: Cherubim as depicted in these images are also referred to as " tetramorphs " because they have faces of a lion, ox, eagle, and man. Cherub is also referenced in the series as a part of "Ode to Joy" (Kaworu's leitmotif). The line "und der Cherub steht vor Gott" ("and the Cherub stands before god") is sung right as Kaworu enters Terminal Dogma, where he believes Adam is located. Cherub is referenced quite a few times in the bible ( complete list ). I believe this is significant: "After he drove the man out, he placed on the east side of the Garden of Eden cherubim and a flaming sword flashing back and forth to guard the way to the tree of life." Genesis 3:24 Note that the tree of life referenced here is the Tree of Life with the apple Adam and Eve ate. The biblical Tree of Life is not very relevant to NGE but the Kabbalistic one is (It appears multiple times throughout the series). It turns out that Cherubim corresponds to Yesod in Kabbalistic angelic hierarchy . Yesod is a sephirah, (in simple terms:) one of the 10 attributes of god according to Kabbala. Properties of Yesod: (From this point on things get messy and incomplete. So many things to read etc. You can research about each property yourself if you want to know more about them. I apologize.) - translates spiritual concepts into actions that unite us with God (Instrumentality) - often associated with the Moon (Rei-Moon) - likened to the 'engine-room' of creation, in that it is an unconscious driving force (S2 Engine, it provides Angels with power but they are not particularly intelligent) - associated with the sexual organs - Cherubim is the Angelic order Many eyed Cherub: A forced connection Cherubim also translates to "Strong Ones". In NGE angels are physically stronger due to their AT fields. Another forced connection. So, what do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Sep 12, 2012 6:53 pm Don't think there's a specific thread about the 12-winged but it's mentioned in a lot of discussion if you want to look. Can do a search for "Sammael" to get some posts.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Sep 12, 2012 9:33 pm it's been a while since I've found something that's completely new to me in this forum. I always thought of the Cherubs as they are depicted in art - you know, the child-like angels. A friend of mine explained this version of Cherubs to a few days ago (in an discussion about Evangelion, no less!), but I hadn't made this connection until now. Thanks!
